ddls:
CREATE TABLE "market_data" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each record */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the data entry */,
  "class" INTEGER /* Class label (e.g., 0 for low volume, 1 for high volume) */,
  "volume" REAL /* Market volume on the given date */,
  "google_trend" INTEGER /* Google search trend value on the given date */,
  "reddit_positive" REAL /* Positive sentiment score from Reddit on the given date */,
  "reddit_negative" REAL /* Negative sentiment score from Reddit on the given date */,
  "google_positive" REAL /* Positive sentiment score from Google on the given date */,
  "google_negative" REAL /* Negative sentiment score from Google on the given date */,
  "reddit_buzzword_score" REAL /* Buzzword score from Reddit on the given date */,
  "google_buzzword_score" REAL /* Buzzword score from Google on the given date */,
  "ticker" TEXT /* Stock ticker symbol for which the data is recorded */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level or reliability score of the data */,
  "volume_change" REAL /* Percentage change in market volume compared to the previous day */,
  "trend_change" REAL /* Percentage change in Google search trend value compared to the previous day */,
  "sentiment_change" REAL /* Change in sentiment scores (positive and negative) compared to the previous day */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_data_source_id FOREIGN KEY ("source_id") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., trader, analyst, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* User status (e.g., active, inactive, suspended) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., Google Trends, Reddit) */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of data source (e.g., search, social media) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "api_endpoint" TEXT /* API endpoint for the data source */,
  "last_synced_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last data sync from the source */,
  "sync_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of data synchronization (e.g., daily, hourly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_source_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source the metric is associated with */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., Positive Sentiment, Negative Sentiment) */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of the metric (e.g., sentiment, buzzword) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the metric */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the metric (e.g., percentage, count) */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the metric to trigger alerts */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "market_predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the prediction */,
  "predicted_volume" REAL /* Predicted market volume */,
  "confidence_score" REAL /* Confidence score of the prediction */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the prediction model used */,
  "actual_volume" REAL /* Actual market volume for the predicted date (for comparison) */,
  "error_margin" REAL /* Difference between predicted and actual volumes */,
  "prediction_model_version" TEXT /* Version of the prediction model used */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the prediction was made */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of the access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data or prediction accessed */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the device used for access */,
  "access_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access log was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_id") REFERENCES market_predictions ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_source_configs" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration setting */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data source the configuration is associated with */,
  "config_name" TEXT /* Name of the configuration setting */,
  "config_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration setting */,
  "last_updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the configuration setting */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the configuration setting is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., trader, analyst, admin) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., view, edit, delete) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role and its responsibilities */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)
